description:
You are on-call as a DevOps engineer who has been asked to quickly identify and block the IP
    addresses responsible for the most recent SSH-brute-force attempts against the host.

    The security team already placed yesterday’s raw SSH log at
    “/home/user/app/logs/access.log”.  
    1. Scan that file for every log line that contains the exact phrase  
       “Failed password”.
    2. Extract only the source IPv4 address from those lines.
       • Ignore duplicate addresses (each address must appear just once).  
       • Sort the addresses in ascending (lexicographic) order.
    3. Write the results to the file
       “/home/user/app/security/failed_ssh_ips.txt”.  
       If the “security” directory does not exist, create it first.

    Output-file format (automated tests will check **exactly**):
        Line 1 : literal text “Blocked IPs:” (without the quotes)  
        Line 2-N: one IPv4 address per line, sorted, with a trailing newline
                  after the final address line.

    After you populate the file, tighten its permissions so that only the
    current user can read and write it (octal mode 600).

    A correct solution produces a file whose
        • path   :  /home/user/app/security/failed_ssh_ips.txt  
        • mode   :  -rw-------  
        • content:
            Blocked IPs:
            192.0.2.10
            198.51.100.23
            203.0.113.7
    (The final newline after “203.0.113.7” is required.)

    You will need no more than 2 – 3 shell commands to complete the task.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing environment for automated testing -->
    <!-- Directory structure -->
    /home/user/app/                : exists, owned by user, mode 755
    /home/user/app/logs/           : exists, owned by user, mode 755

    <!-- Pre-existing log file with brute-force entries -->
    /home/user/app/logs/access.log : mode 644, contents exactly:
        Aug 11 15:12:32 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.0.2.10 port 51158 ssh2
        Aug 11 15:13:00 server sshd[1235]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 51234 ssh2
        Aug 11 15:13:15 server sshd[1236]: Failed password for invalid user test from 192.0.2.10 port 51376 ssh2
        Aug 11 15:14:22 server sshd[1237]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.7 port 51455 ssh2
        Aug 11 15:14:50 server sshd[1238]: Accepted password for alice from 198.51.100.34 port 51477 ssh2
        Aug 11 15:15:05 server sshd[1239]: Connection closed by authenticating user bob 203.0.113.8 port 51501 [preauth]

    <!-- Expected artefact after agent finishes -->
    /home/user/app/security/                : must exist, mode 755 (directory)
    /home/user/app/security/failed_ssh_ips.txt
        mode        : 600 (-rw-------)
        contents    :
            Blocked IPs:
            192.0.2.10
            198.51.100.23
            203.0.113.7
            [final newline]
name: task_000000_54766b2d
